claim: Bitcoin is more likely than not to be above $70,000 on the relevant April 20 Binance noon-ET 1-minute close, but the swarm’s below-market lean remains the better synthesis after verification: current spot around 74.1k and clear contract mechanics support Yes, yet a ~5-6% drawdown over five days plus exact-minute settlement fragility make 87% look somewhat rich rather than clearly fair.

frontmatter.contract_ambiguity_reason: exact Binance noon-ET 1m candle handling is clear in rules but still operationally minute-specific
independently_verified_points: Polymarket rules explicitly settle on Binance BTC/USDT 12:00 ET 1-minute close above 70000
Live Binance BTCUSDT was about 74130-74150 during synthesis check
Recent Binance daily regime was mostly above 70000
March 2026 CPI already passed on Apr 10 so no obvious major CPI release remains before Apr 20
frontmatter.verification_gap_summary: No strong independent check converted current cushion into a precise five-day breach probability.
frontmatter.best_countercase_summary: The market may be roughly right because BTC only needs to avoid a moderate drawdown and is already trading materially above 70k.
frontmatter.main_reason_for_disagreement: weight placed on five-day volatility and single-minute settlement fragility versus spot-distance heuristics
frontmatter.resolution_mechanics_summary: Yes resolves only if the Binance BTC/USDT 12:00 ET Apr 20 1-minute candle final close is strictly above 70000.
frontmatter.freshness_driver: BTC short-horizon price path into the Apr 20 12:00 ET Binance settlement minute
decision_blockers: Short-horizon BTC volatility could erase the current 4.1k cushion
Single-minute Binance settlement adds path and microstructure fragility
No rigorous independent volatility model was run to validate a large edge versus market
frontmatter.next_checkpoint: Recheck Binance BTCUSDT and catalyst state on Apr 19-20, especially if BTC moves toward 72k or below.

sections.Alpha summary: Market implies 0.87 Yes. My post-synthesis range is 0.80 to 0.85 Yes. That leaves at most a marginal No-side edge versus market, not a huge one. The likely mispricing, if any, is that the market slightly underweights five-day BTC volatility and exact-minute Binance settlement fragility.
sections.Input coverage: All five personas were available and usable: base-rate, catalyst-hunter, market-implied, risk-manager, and variant-view. No personas were missing. I critically compared sidecars against raw findings; the sidecars appeared broadly faithful rather than distorted. Supporting assumption/evidence artifacts were not needed heavily because the raw findings themselves were sufficiently detailed. Coverage is complete.
sections.Market-implied baseline: Baseline market-implied probability is 0.87 as of the dispatch snapshot. The swarm center was about 0.81, with all personas below market but still Yes-lean.
sections.Syndicated probability estimate: My final estimate is 0.80 to 0.85 Yes. That keeps Yes favored because current Binance spot is around 74.1k and recent regime evidence is mostly above 70k, but it stops short of endorsing the full 0.87 market confidence because the contract resolves on one exact Binance minute five days out.
sections.Difference from swarm-implied center: This is only slightly above the swarm-implied center near 0.81. I moved a bit upward because synthesis-stage verification supported the strongest pro-Yes facts: contract mechanics are clear, live Binance price remained around 74.1k, and no obvious major scheduled CPI catalyst remains before resolution. I did not move all the way to market because the extra research did not independently verify that the remaining downside probability is as low as 13%.
sections.Agreement or disagreement with market: I still disagree modestly with market. The market is directionally right that Yes is favored, but 0.87 looks somewhat rich for a five-day crypto threshold contract settled on one exchange-minute. The disagreement is about confidence, not direction.
sections.Independent verification of edge: Verification quality is medium. I independently checked the governing Polymarket rules, current Binance BTCUSDT price, recent Binance 1-minute and daily data, and the BLS CPI schedule. That was enough to verify the contract mechanics, current cushion, and the absence of one obvious scheduled macro catalyst. What remained weak was independent quantification of how often BTC loses ~5.5% within five days under comparable conditions; without that, the edge versus market cannot be rated highly verified.
sections.Compression toward market due to verific: Yes. The raw swarm leaned meaningfully below market, roughly centered near 0.81. Because synthesis verification confirmed the current spot cushion and did not uncover a strong near-term scheduled bearish catalyst, I compressed partway back toward market to 0.80-0.85 rather than endorsing a lower range like 0.78-0.82. The remaining below-market lean survives because volatility/path risk was not independently disproven.
sections.Timing and catalyst posture: The key checkpoint is the path into Apr 20 noon ET. This edge is likely to decay or compress toward the prevailing spot-distance heuristic if BTC remains comfortably above 72k-73k into Apr 19-20. Waiting can improve accuracy because freshness matters a lot here, but it also reduces any tradable edge if price remains stable.
sections.Decision blockers: There is no major contract blocker; mechanics are mostly clear. The real blockers are short-horizon volatility, exact-minute settlement fragility, and the lack of a stronger independent model translating current cushion into true five-day breach odds. So the blocker is confidence, not interpretive paralysis.
sections.Implication for the question: The best current answer is still Yes-lean, but not near-lock Yes. BTC being above 70k right now is supportive, yet the question is specifically about one Binance 1-minute close on Apr 20 at noon ET, so a moderate selloff or brief settlement-minute weakness can still flip the result.
sections.Consensus across personas: All personas agreed that Yes is more likely than No. All agreed the governing source of truth is the Binance BTC/USDT 12:00 ET 1-minute candle close on Apr 20. All agreed current spot near 74.1k provides a meaningful but not overwhelming cushion. All agreed the main reason to sit below or near market is exact-minute settlement fragility plus plausible 5-6% BTC downside over five days.
sections.Key disagreements across personas: Main disagreement was market-pricing/weighting-based rather than factual. The market-implied persona viewed 0.87 as broadly defensible and only slightly rich. Base-rate and risk-manager were more skeptical, emphasizing that a 5-6% move over five days is common enough to keep fair value in the high 70s rather than high 80s. Catalyst-hunter was somewhat more constructive because the visible scheduled calendar looked light. Variant-view emphasized cross-venue confirmation but still discounted confidence due to exchange-minute path dependence.
sections.Best countercase: Best countercase: the market may already be close to right because BTC is trading materially above 70k, recent regime evidence is supportive, and no obvious major scheduled catalyst remains before settlement. The market-implied persona represented this best.
sections.Encapsulated assumptions: Shared assumptions: Binance remains operationally normal; current ~74.1k level is a meaningful anchor; no large downside shock arrives before Apr 20; ET-to-UTC mapping is straightforward. Contested assumptions: whether current cushion should imply something near 84-87% versus high-70s/low-80s; how much weight to place on unscheduled shock risk. Fragile assumptions: that no Binance-specific wick or brief dislocation appears at the exact settlement minute.
sections.Encapsulated evidence map: Strongest supporting evidence: Polymarket rules clearly define settlement; live Binance spot around 74.1k leaves ~4.1k cushion; recent Binance daily closes were mostly above 70k. Strongest contradictory evidence: BTC can drop ~5-6% in days; this is a single-minute contract on one venue; exact-minute path dependence matters more than broad directional bullishness. Governing source-of-truth evidence was strong; forecasting evidence was more inferential than directly modeled.
sections.Evidence weighting: Most weight went to direct settlement mechanics and direct Binance market-state checks. Moderate weight went to the catalyst calendar check showing CPI already passed. Less weight went to broad narrative arguments or generic crypto sentiment. I downweighted any inference that current spot cushion alone justifies near-certainty.
sections.Counterpoints / strongest disconfirming: The strongest disconfirming evidence against a high-Yes view is not a specific bearish news item but the base-rate fact that BTC can fall 5-6% inside five days without much warning. Because the contract is settled on one exact Binance minute, even a brief drawdown or venue-specific wick could resolve No despite an otherwise healthy weekly tape.
sections.Resolution or source-of-truth interpreta: Resolution mechanics are mostly clear and were independently checked. Yes requires the final close of the Binance BTC/USDT 1-minute candle for 12:00 ET on Apr 20 to be strictly above 70,000. Equality does not qualify. Other exchanges, daily closes, and broader BTC averages do not matter. Ambiguity is minor rather than none because minute-specific operational handling always deserves caution, but there is no major wording dispute.
sections.Why this could create or destroy alpha: Alpha exists only if the market is slightly too complacent about path dependence. If the crowd is anchoring too hard on current spot and not enough on five-day downside variance plus one-minute settlement mechanics, No may be a small-value side. But because synthesis verification also confirmed the supportive spot cushion and lack of an obvious scheduled catalyst, the edge is likely modest rather than dramatic.
sections.What would falsify this interpretation /: A stable hold above roughly 72k-73k into Apr 19-20 with low realized volatility would push the view closer to or even up to market. A break toward 72k, 71k, or below would push the estimate down materially. Any Binance-specific operational anomaly or clearer evidence on settlement-minute handling would also matter disproportionately.
sections.Highest-value next research: Highest-value next research is a closer-to-settlement check of Binance BTCUSDT versus 70k, including realized volatility and any venue-specific dislocation risk on Apr 19-20. If doing one deeper quant pass, model historical five-day downside probability from a similar starting cushion.
sections.Source-quality assessment: Primary source class was strong: Polymarket rules plus direct Binance data. Important secondary/contextual source class was macro release scheduling. Evidence independence was medium: the settlement stack and price context are both tightly linked to Binance. Source-of-truth ambiguity was low to minor. The synthesis is somewhat bottlenecked not by missing facts, but by limited independent quant modeling of breach probability.
sections.Verification impact: Yes, synthesis used extra verification beyond persona comparison: fresh Polymarket and Binance checks plus a BLS schedule check. Cross-lane comparison materially narrowed the interpretation: sidecars were faithful, no persona surfaced a major hidden contract issue, and the main live question remained confidence calibration rather than fact discovery. The verification pass raised confidence in the pro-Yes baseline but also failed to justify trusting the full market price.
sections.Persona contribution map: base-rate — strongest outside-view warning that a 5-6% crypto move in five days is common enough to keep fair value below market. catalyst-hunter — strongest case that the visible scheduled macro calendar is relatively light before resolution. market-implied — best articulation of why the market’s high Yes price is not obviously irrational. risk-manager — clearest framing of one-minute settlement fragility and confidence haircut logic. variant-view — useful independent cross-venue price-level check and clean statement of exchange-specific minute-risk.
sections.Reusable lesson signals: Durable lesson: single-minute exchange-specific crypto threshold contracts deserve a confidence discount relative to plain spot-distance intuition. Possible underbuilt driver: explicit short-horizon breach-probability modeling from current cushion. Source-quality lesson: direct exchange checks are necessary but not sufficient for validating large edges versus market. Reusability confidence: medium.
sections.Orchestrator review suggestions: review later for durable lesson: yes; review later for driver candidate: yes; review later for canon or linkage issue: no; review later for swarm-method issue: yes. Reason: this case repeatedly surfaced a method gap between direct contract/spot verification and explicit short-horizon volatility calibration.
sections.Recommended follow-up: Wait for a closer-to-settlement refresh unless a decision is needed immediately. If acting now, treat any edge as small and verification-limited. If rerunning, focus on Apr 19-20 Binance spot cushion, realized volatility, and any sign of exchange-minute dislocation rather than redoing basic contract interpretation.
